health_zone_table.basis: SitRep47 Table 2 (30 June 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF and reconciled to Table 1 / province totals 1406/438. A new affected health zone (Lolwa, Ituri) is reported, raising the affected-zone count to 36/104. The table carries 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified; these remain an unventilated residual and are not distributed to named health zones. Imported cases in Tshopo and Haut-Uele provinces are counted in the Nia-Nia health zone (Ituri). PDF sha256 a7596c30ac0794f5f2b5b1242daa767f6963d56facb531afc10f61eec9191ec6.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1389 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation = 1406; all 438 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep47 section 4.3: Ituri 109 collected/analyzed / 69 positive; Nord-Kivu 135 collected/analyzed / 4 positive; Sud-Kivu 1 collected and under analysis (0 analyzed). Positivity uses 73 positives over 244 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 609 patients at 92.7% global occupancy (unchanged from SitRep46). SitRep47 published no patient-movement table, so the confirmed/suspected isolation split (216 confirmed, 393 suspected) is carried forward from SitRep46 and flagged as not freshly reported.
From the Faits saillants, 19 patients were declared recovered in Ituri and 14 deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients (9 Ituri, 5 Nord-Kivu) during the cycle.
highlights: 73 new confirmed cases including 25 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-30, in Ituri (69 including 22 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (4 including 3 deaths).

New confirmed cases were distributed across Bunia (29), Nizi (20), Rwampara (6), Mongbwalu (6), Komanda (2), Nyankunde (1), Tchomia (1), Mandima (1), Nia-Nia (1), Kambala (1) and Lolwa (1) in Ituri, and Katwa (2) and Beni (2) in Nord-Kivu.
A new affected health zone, Lolwa (Ituri), was reported on 2026-06-30, raising affected zones to 36/104; Ituri now reports 24/36 zones touched.

Investigations of 29 June events notified two confirmed cases in Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele province; one death and one living case that fled from Nia-Nia) and one confirmed case in Tshopo province (a 24-year-old pregnant woman who died at PK 51, the Nia-Nia epicenter, whose body was moved clandestinely to Kisangani where a sample tested positive). All were attached to and counted within the Nia-Nia health zone (Abanagomu health area), Ituri.

14 additional deaths were recorded among previously confirmed CTE patients (9 in Ituri: 5 CTE Bunia, 2 CTE Mongbwalu, 1 CT Bambu, 1 CTE Elikya; 5 in Nord-Kivu: 4 CTE Katwa, 1 CTE Beni), bringing confirmed deaths to 438. 19 patients recovered in Ituri, bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 208.
laboratory: SitRep47 reports Ituri collected and analyzed 109 samples with 69 positive (63.3%).
Nord-Kivu collected and analyzed 135 samples with 4 positive (3.0%). Sud-Kivu collected 1 sample, still under analysis.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 47 reports through 2026-06-30 and was published on the INSP WordPress site (post 25073; PDF media 25074; WP publish 2026-07-02T04:50:22Z). The PDF states a publication date of 1 July 2026.
SitRep47 supersedes SitRep46 directly (a single report-day advance); the cumulative rose by 73 confirmed and 39 deaths over 2026-06-30.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 36 of 104 health zones; Lolwa (Ituri) is the newly affected zone this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1283 of 1406 DRC confirmed cases (91.2%).
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 59.2%, led by Katwa (70.2%), Oicha (66.7%), Kyondo (66.7%) and Beni (62.5%); 100% lethality observed in low-count Vuhovi.
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1,234 alerts were reported and 1,035 investigated, producing an 83.9% investigation rate; Ituri 52.1% (212/407), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (814/814), Sud-Kivu 69.2% (9/13).
301 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 90 deceased validated alerts.
11,646 contacts were under follow-up and 9,605 were seen, producing 82.5% follow-up coverage (completeness 90.6%; Gety and Mandima in Ituri and Musienene in Nord-Kivu had no data at consolidation).

389 new contacts were listed (353 across 8 Ituri health zones and 36 across 2 Nord-Kivu health zones); 104 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (83 in Ituri, 31 in Nord-Kivu). A multidisciplinary team was deployed to Tshopo and Haut-Uele and some contacts were transferred to Nia-Nia for follow-up continuity.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 22; new alerts 1212; total alerts of the day 1234. 389 new contacts listed (353 in 8 Ituri HZs, 36 in 2 Nord-Kivu HZs); 104 completed 21-day follow-up (83 Ituri, 31 Nord-Kivu).

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table (SitRep47 published no patient-movement table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 92.7%), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 82.5% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion, underscored this cycle by imported cases in Tshopo and Haut-Uele traced back to Nia-Nia.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Follow-up rate computed only for reporting ZS (completeness 90.6%); Gety and Mandima (Ituri) and Musienene (Nord-Kivu) had no data at consolidation. Sud-Kivu contacts remain exited. 389 new contacts listed (353 in 8 Ituri HZs, 36 in 2 Nord-Kivu HZs); 104 completed 21-day follow-up (83 Ituri, 31 Nord-Kivu). A multidisciplinary team was deployed to Tshopo and Haut-Uele and some contacts were transferred to Nia-Nia for follow-up continuity.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep47 published no patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table; the banner reports only the aggregate isolation census 609 at 92.7% occupancy, unchanged from SitRep46. The confirmed/suspected split (216/393) is carried forward from SitRep46 as last reported. Only two exit flows are sourced from the Faits saillants this cycle: 19 recoveries (all Ituri) and 14 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (9 Ituri, 5 Nord-Kivu); other 24h movement flows were not published and are shown as 0.

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep Tshopo/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia
Relieve isolation load at 92.7% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 59.2% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
Improve contact follow-up (82.5% vs 95% target), sustain the new Lolwa focus, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog

preserved_conflicts: SitRep47 headline is DRC-only (1406 confirmed / 438 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

The 1,406 confirmed total is marked re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning/reclassification/harmonization; SitRep47 supersedes SitRep46 directly (a single report-day advance), so treat the +73 confirmed and +39 confirmed deaths vs SitRep46 as an official one-day delta for 2026-06-30.
SitRep47 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.

A new affected health zone, Lolwa (Ituri), is reported with 1 confirmed / 1 death; affected zones advance from 35 to 36 of 104. New map geometry/centroid was added for Lolwa this cycle (GRID3 polygon area-centroid).

Imported cases in Tshopo and Haut-Uele provinces are administratively counted within the Nia-Nia health zone (Ituri): two Wamba ZS (Haut-Uele) cases (one death, one living case that fled from Nia-Nia) and one Tshopo case (a 24-year-old pregnant woman who died at PK 51, the Nia-Nia epicenter, whose body was moved clandestinely to Kisangani where a sample tested positive), all attached to the Abanagomu health area of Nia-Nia. The affected-province count remains 3; there is no separate Haut-Uele or Tshopo denominator.

The isolation census (609 at 92.7% occupancy) is reported only in the SitRep47 banner and is unchanged from SitRep46; SitRep47 published no patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table, so the confirmed/suspected isolation split (216 confirmed, 393 suspected) and 24h movement flows are carried forward from SitRep46 (2026-06-29) and flagged as not freshly reported. From the Faits saillants, 19 recoveries and 14 CTE deaths occurred this cycle.
Daily suspected cases (301) and the 90 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard (last reviewed 2026-06-21); carried forward this cycle as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1426 confirmed / 440 deaths / 219 recovered.

Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26, suggesting no recent transmission; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death across Miti-Murhesa, and its contacts remain exited from follow-up.
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality this cycle (Lolwa 1/1, Kambala 2/2, Drodro 3/3, Vuhovi 1/1); these are unstable small-number ratios.

review_notes: SitRep #047 (report date 2026-06-30; INSP WordPress post 25073 published 2026-07-02T04:50:22Z; embedded PDF media 25074). PDF sha256 a7596c30ac0794f5f2b5b1242daa767f6963d56facb531afc10f61eec9191ec6, 12 pages. Title N047/MVB_30/06/2026; the PDF states a publication date of 1 July 2026; treated as report-date 2026-06-30.

SitRep47 supersedes SitRep46 directly (single report-day). DRC headline 1406 confirmed / 438 confirmed deaths (CFR 31.2%); +73 confirmed and +39 confirmed deaths vs SitRep46. On 30 June, 73 new confirmed (Ituri 69, Nord-Kivu 4) carried 25 deaths; a further 14 deaths occurred among previously confirmed CTE patients (9 Ituri, 5 Nord-Kivu).
The 1,406 total is flagged re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning/reclassification; deltas are official revised deltas.

A new affected health zone, Lolwa (Ituri), is reported (1 confirmed / 1 death). Affected-zone count advances from 35 to 36 of 104; Ituri now 24/36 touched. New map geometry/centroid was added for Lolwa this cycle (GRID3 polygon area-centroid from the served region-health-zones.geojson).
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1406 confirmed / 438 deaths: 1389 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 438 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Imported Tshopo and Haut-Uele cases are counted within Nia-Nia (Ituri): two Wamba/Haut-Uele cases (one death, one living case that fled from Nia-Nia) and one Tshopo case (pregnant woman who died at PK 51, sample positive in Kisangani), all attached to the Abanagomu health area of Nia-Nia. The affected-province count stays at 3 and there is no separate Haut-Uele or Tshopo denominator.

Recovered advanced to 208 DRC cumulative (+19, all Ituri). Uganda anchor carried forward at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (last reviewed 2026-06-21), so country-scope is 1426 confirmed / 440 deaths / 219 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 109 collected/analyzed, 69 positive (63.3%); Nord-Kivu 135 collected/analyzed, 4 positive (3.0%); Sud-Kivu 1 collected/under analysis (244 analyzed / 73 positive overall, 29.9%).

Operational data captured: Table 3 alerts 1,234 reported / 1,035 investigated (83.9%; narrative consistent), 301 suspected cases of the day (90 deceased validated alerts), 11,646 contacts under follow-up (82.5%), 389 new contacts, PoC activation 68/69 with 230,008 travelers passed. SitRep47 published no patient-movement table; the isolation census (609, 92.7%) and confirmed/suspected split (216/393) are carried from SitRep46 and flagged.
